SEARCH
Package microsim.ui

Class DebugShell

java.lang.Object
microsim.ui.DebugShell
All Implemented Interfaces:
SimulationListener
public class DebugShell extends Object implements SimulationListener
Handles the shell shown in debug mode. Is a singleton.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Class
    Description

    DebugShell.DebugExceptionHandler
    Declares a custom exception handler for uncaught exceptions.

    Enum to index help pages.

    Method
    Description
    void
    activate()
    Activates debug shell.

    Attaches a new simulation instance to the debug shell (basically makes self a listener and takes a reference to show on demand information from shell).

    Deactivates debug shell.

    Prints EPROM data array word by word.

    Prints all active instances, including index mappings and power state.

    Fetches MicroOp queue from processor in simulation at index and prints it.

    Fetches processor registers from processor in simulation at index and prints them.

    Reads and prints data from memory at given address from simulation at index.

    Starts the world.

  • Field Details

    • singleton

      private static DebugShell singleton
      The only allowed instance of this class.

    • debuggingEnabled

      private static volatile boolean debuggingEnabled
      Static flag that signals whether debugging is enabled. This is static as it's used by components to decide whether to raise debug events or not (raising them anyways is inefficient).

    • isWorldStopped

      private static volatile boolean isWorldStopped
      Static flag that signals whether the world is stopped. This is static as the first debug shell instance that stops the world stops it for all simulation instances.

    • remainingCycles

      private long remainingCycles
      Cycle counter for multi-cycle stepping.

    • cycleInstance

      private long cycleInstance
      Keeps track of which instance we are multi-cycle stepping on.

    • shouldGreet

      private static volatile boolean shouldGreet
      Flag that signals whether the shell should greet the user. Set to false at first greet to have it greet on startup.

    • simulationInstances

      private final List<Simulation> simulationInstances
      Simulation instances to debug. Used to access processor and memory information from shell.

    • eventQueues

      private final List<Queue<SimulationEvent>> eventQueues
      Event queues per simulation. Keep track of the events a simulation emitted: these will be displayed at the next cycle event of that simulation.

  • Constructor Details

    • DebugShell

      private DebugShell()
      Hide constructor.

  • Method Details

    • getInstance

      public static DebugShell getInstance()
      Returns the singleton debug shell.
      Returns:
      singleton shell

    • isDebuggingEnabled

      public static boolean isDebuggingEnabled()
      Returns whether debugging is enabled.
      Returns:
      is debugging enabled?

    • activate

      public void activate()
      Activates debug shell.

    • deactivate

      public void deactivate()
      Deactivates debug shell.

    • stopTheWorld

      private static void stopTheWorld()
      Stops the world.

    • startTheWorld

      private static void startTheWorld()
      Starts the world.

    • isWorldStopped

      public static boolean isWorldStopped()
      Returns whether world is stopped. ThreadedIoDevice devices are meant to check this, via the ThreadedIoDevice.smartSpin(long) method, to correctly stop when asked to.
      Returns:
      is world stopped?

    • attachSimulation

      public void attachSimulation(Simulation simulationInstance)
      Attaches a new simulation instance to the debug shell (basically makes self a listener and takes a reference to show on demand information from shell).
      Parameters:
      simulationInstance - simulation instance to attach

    • int32ToString

      public static String int32ToString(int val)
      Helper to convert an int to a hexadecimal string. Used by this class and as an utility to build DebugEvent messages. This method alone is fine for most messages as all RV32I registers are 32 bit.
      Parameters:
      val - int to convert
      Returns:
      hexadecimal hex string representing int

    • printEPROM

      public static void printEPROM(byte[] eprom)
      Prints EPROM data array word by word. Used as an utility by whoever gets data from ELF.
      Parameters:
      eprom - EPROM data array

    • printProcessorRegisters

      private void printProcessorRegisters(int idx)
      Fetches processor registers from processor in simulation at index and prints them.
      Parameters:
      idx - index of simulation

    • printProcessorMicroOps

      private void printProcessorMicroOps(int idx)
      Fetches MicroOp queue from processor in simulation at index and prints it.
      Parameters:
      idx - index of simulation

    • printInstances

      private void printInstances()
      Prints all active instances, including index mappings and power state.

    • readMemoryAtAddress

      private void readMemoryAtAddress(int idx, String addr)
      Reads and prints data from memory at given address from simulation at index. Checks valid addresses and out of bound reads.
      Parameters:
      idx - index of simulation
      addr - the address to read from

    • writeMemoryAtAddress

      private void writeMemoryAtAddress(int idx, String addr, String data)
      Writes data to memory at given address from simulation at index. Checks valid addresses, valid data and out of bound writes.
      Parameters:
      idx - index of simulation
      addr - the address to write at
      data - data to write

    • onSimulationEvent

      public void onSimulationEvent(SimulationEvent e)
      Receives a simulation event.
      Specified by:
      onSimulationEvent in interface SimulationListener
      Parameters:
      e - simulation event

    • log

      private void log(SimulationEvent event)
      Logs a debug event.
      Parameters:
      event - event to log

    • greet

      private void greet()
      Greets the user and gives basic info.

    • help

      private void help(DebugShell.HelpPage page)
      Shows an help page.
      Parameters:
      page - help page to show

    • getSimulationIndex

      private int getSimulationIndex(String idxString)
      Helpers that gets the numerical index of a simulation from a string. Does valid format and bound checking.
      Parameters:
      idxString - string representing simulation index
      Returns:
      actual simulation index

    • shell

      private void shell()
      Displays a debug shell for the current simulation instance.
